subject: Math
motivational_issues: Amelia reached the 99th percentile but did not hit double growth, so her guide is building a plan where she invests 20 minutes per day into Fast Math as a session-long priority, with offline support and app privileges tied to her progress.
academic_issues:
Amelia spent a significant amount of time in Zearn to finish 3rd grade.
- She already knew roughly 70% of 3rd-grade content but we did not have the tools available to fill her knowledge gaps at the beginning of the year. She wasted time on Synthesis and by starting 3rd grade from scratch in Zearn.
-  She is two grades behind in Fast Math compared to her Math grade, which creates a computational gap and slows down her learning.

She is now rostered in 4th grade Math, which is the right level for her, but she still needs to close the fast math gap.
motivational_action_plan: Although 2X was not met, Amelia did hit 99th percentile. In the last week of S3, L1s built out our S4 MM. Subjects where 2X was not met for MAP will become a session long priority app with offline support. Privledges unlocked for XP in math app + MAP focused vocabulary.

subject: Math
motivational_issues: Blair averages only 24 minutes per day in Math, just under the 25-minute daily target, so her guide will deliver her app order every day and require her to complete at least 20 minutes of Math before moving on, regardless of points earned or rings closed, to keep her progression toward doubling her Math MAP score.
academic_issues:
Blair worked below her capabilities for 2 months (August-mid October) because they scored 88% in a G3 placement test but there was no hole filling available at the beginning of the year.
Blair is now correctly placed in Prealgebra and on track to meet their goals.
motivational_action_plan: Blair does not need a carrot for her Math. She was struggling to get unstuck but has overcome her struggles and now is consistently able to get her Math XP. To continue this progression and get her to 2X on the Math MAP test, I will deliver her app order everyday and require her to complete 20 minutes of Math (minimum) before moving on, regardless of her XP or ring closure.

subject: Math
motivational_issues: We will maintain a strong emphasis on encouragement, small wins, and visible progress to build Betty's confidence while her foundational skills develop. By celebrating effort, providing brief coaching when instructions become overwhelming, and reinforcing pattern recognition through repeated exposure, we aim to keep her motivated while gradually increasing her efficiency.
academic_issues: Betty's growth has stalled due to introduction of Pre-K Fast Math in end of January. Betty's lack of fact automaticity created an auditory and working memory bottleneck in Zearn; Betty needs to practice 20 minutes in FastMath daily (divided into 4x5 min sessions across Alpha Numbers and game apps - which we will add) to build the fluency required for Grade K mastery.
motivational_action_plan: We will maintain a strong emphasis on encouragement, small wins, and visible progress to build Betty's confidence while her foundational skills develop. By celebrating effort, providing brief coaching when instructions become overwhelming, and reinforcing pattern recognition through repeated exposure, we aim to keep her motivated while gradually increasing her efficiency.

subject: Reading
motivational_issues: Anthem averaged 22 minutes per day in Reading, 3 minutes below her 25-minute daily target, so she needs to spend at least 25 focused minutes per day in Reading. We are working together to address the overwhelm she experiences with longer texts by practicing breaking them into smaller checkpoints and asking questions at each one.
academic_issues: Anthem passed G2 Reading on March 2, but has not yet met the Grade 2 Oral Reading Fluency (ORF) benchmark required to move up to G3 Reading. They are currently hole-filling in G2 ClearFluency and Alpha Reading Fluency, and must be given regular ORF retests to confirm readiness for advancement to G3 Reading. Testing cadence must be maintained going forward to avoid similar delays.
motivational_action_plan: Anthem has grown tremendously as a reader this year though she becomes overwhelmed by longer texts on tests! We are woking together to overcome the overwhelm by practicing breaking apart larger texts into smaller checkpoints and asking questions at each checkpoint.

subject: Reading
motivational_issues: Luisela is dedicating only 18 minutes per school day to Reading, below the 25-minute daily target, despite earning 30 XP/day. Because she has an approved modified attendance plan of 1 to 2 times per week, she is not held to the standard growth expectation, and her guide's approach reflects that adjusted plan.
academic_issues: Luisela spent her first four months this year working in Anton, which did not yield meaningful improvements in her reading fluency. After transitioning to ClearFluency, she has shown gains. She will continue in ClearFluency Grade 1 until she passes the Grade 1 Oral Reading Fluency benchmark.
motivational_action_plan: Luisela has an approved, modified attendance plan (1-2X/week).  She does not have the expectation to hit 2X.

subject: Reading
motivational_issues:
The projected growth was: 2x
Their actual growth was: 1.6x
To get them into that bucket, the plan is to leverage resources attained from Dr. Kath to improve A'Leiah's performance on Clearfluency, and reward her for reaching ambitious goals in terms of the amount of books she completes in the app, as well as the amount of badges she earns in Lalilo. Also included in that motivational model will be rewards for completing independent reading at home or during study hall with the intent of helping to build her reading stamina.
academic_issues: A'Leiah's limited growth was due to a foundational phonics and decoding gap. The 1.5 min/XP reflects the additional processing time complex decoding content requires. A'Leiah has been rostered in AlphaLiteracy as her primary reading program, replacing current supplemental apps, to provide explicit phonics instruction targeting decoding skills.
motivational_action_plan:
The projected growth was: 2x
Their actual growth was: 1.6x
To get them into that bucket, the plan is to leverage resources attained from Dr. Kath to improve A'Leiah's performance on Clearfluency, and reward her for reaching ambitious goals in terms of the amount of books she completes in the app, as well as the amount of badges she earns in Lalilo. Also included in that motivational model will be rewards for completing independent reading at home or during study hall with the intent of helping to build her reading stamina.

subject: Reading
motivational_issues: King averages 23 minutes/day in Reading, below the 25-minute daily target. He wastes ~20% of learning time by rushing through quizzes without reading responses and skipping instructional material. He also has an outstanding oral reading fluency test that needs to be taken to determine optimal placement. He needs to spend at least 25 focused minutes per day, review instructional videos/passages before answering quiz questions, and take the pending assessment.
academic_issues: The 6+ MAP Reading test that King takes does not have audio support, but the student has trouble reading independently. He struggles to comprehend the passages in these tests, and must continue to work towards better reading fluency and comprehension to be able to demonstrate growth.

subject: Language
motivational_issues: Arianna averages only 18 minutes per day in Language, below the 25-minute daily target, and her overall growth has been low across subjects due to consistency and efficiency gaps; her guide will use a sticker chart with end-of-block check-ins to track daily progress, structure shorter focused work sprints, and reward her with additional one-on-one guide lunches when she meets her daily goals.
academic_issues: Arianna was misplaced in G1 Language from August to January — she was rostered in Grade 1 after scoring 75% on the G1 placement, but then tested out of G1 and G2 with 100%, indicating she could have been working on more challenging content. She is now correctly placed in G3. No further action required.
motivational_action_plan: Arianna's low growth (0.4–1.0X) and high minutes per XP (42 in Math) indicate a consistency and efficiency gap; she will track daily XP and accuracy by subject using a sticker chart with end-of-block check-ins. To move her toward 2X growth, I will structure shorter, clearly defined work sprints and reinforce sustained focus by earning additional 1:1 guide lunches tied to meeting daily XP expectations.

subject: Science
motivational_issues: Rhys averaged 11 minutes per day in Science, falling short of the 12.5-minute daily target, and struggles to grasp high-level concepts. To close this gap, the guide will encourage Rhys to restate difficult ideas in his own words and reward the creation of diagrams and visual tools to strengthen his understanding.
academic_issues: Rhys exhibits fragile mastery in specific Life Science strands, including genetics, natural selection, and ecosystem energy flow, requiring as many as 6 attempts to master individual skills. Academics has assigned targeted hole-filling in MobyMax to bridge these gaps and ensure transferable understanding.
motivational_action_plan: Rhys struggles with science concepts, so I will encourage him to restate high-level ideas in his own words. I will also incentivize creating diagrams and other visual tools to help him better understand challenging concepts.

subject: Science
motivational_issues: Jacob averages only 15 minutes/day in Science, well below the 25-minute daily target. He must consistently spend at least 25 minutes per day on Science.
academic_issues: Jacob spent the first four months of the year in content below his capabilities — he scored 80% in Grade 3 during placement but was assigned the full G3 course instead of hole-filling lessons. His 0.4 min/XP reflects the lack of challenge for most of the content he worked on this year. He is now working in Grade 8, and no further academic action is required.
